entries.title: Evacuation Students Are Forced Into War Consequences

context.label: Students assist evacuation before combat escalates

coordinates.label: Civilian evacuation and student rear guard
Hero society in open war with PLF

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Students assigned to evacuation and rear support are still drawn into the war’s consequences once Shigaraki and Gigantomachia break containment.
content.injection: Student scenes should retain the shock of escalation. They begin as support personnel and are forced into disaster response.

content.fact: Midnight, injured and separated, directs students toward a sedative strategy against Gigantomachia before being overwhelmed.
content.injection: When Midnight falls, frame her final instruction as a teacher passing responsibility to students under impossible conditions.

content.fact: Momo organizes a sedative delivery plan under extreme time pressure, converting fear into logistics and coordination.
content.injection: Write Momo as frightened but functional. Her value is planning, material creation, and command under pressure.

entries.title: Kirishima Completes A Courage Arc Against Machia

context.label: Students attempt sedative plan against Gigantomachia

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Kirishima’s attempt to deliver the sedative against Gigantomachia echoes and answers his earlier fear of freezing in danger.
content.injection: Use Kirishima’s Machia moment as courage under terror, not simple toughness. His action matters because he chooses to move anyway.

entries.title: Mina Freezes Because Machia Triggers Earlier Trauma

context.label: Students attempt sedative plan against Gigantomachia

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mina briefly freezes against Gigantomachia because his presence recalls her earlier encounter before U.A., making the failure emotionally specific.
content.injection: Do not write Mina’s hesitation as incompetence. It is trauma-triggered and immediately relevant to Kirishima’s follow-through.

entries.title: Gigantomachia Is A Moving Disaster Zone

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Gigantomachia’s march functions like a mobile disaster, crushing terrain, buildings, and evacuation assumptions as he moves toward Shigaraki.
content.injection: Treat Machia as catastrophe-scale. Scenes around him should involve shockwaves, route destruction, impossible size, and evacuation collapse.

entries.title: Mt Lady And Other Pros Can Slow Machia But Not Stop Him Alone

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mt. Lady and other heroes physically delay Gigantomachia, but individual strength cannot fully halt him once he is moving.
content.injection: When pros engage Machia, frame their work as delay and damage reduction, not an easy physical contest.

entries.title: Sedative Success Is Delayed Rather Than Immediate

context.label: Students attempt sedative plan against Gigantomachia

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The students’ sedative matters, but it does not instantly drop Gigantomachia at the moment of delivery.
content.injection: Do not make the sedative an immediate off-switch. Its delayed effect preserves the march’s devastation and later collapse.

entries.title: Machia Carries The League Toward Shigaraki

content.fact: Gigantomachia carries League members toward Shigaraki, merging the villa and hospital fronts into one larger catastrophe.
content.injection: After Machia moves, prepare for fronts to converge. League members physically arrive through Machia, not through clean tactical redeployment.

entries.title: Students See The Gap Between Training And War

context.label: Students attempt sedative plan against Gigantomachia

content.fact: The rear-guard students confront a scale of violence far beyond exams, festivals, and controlled training.
content.injection: In student war scenes, show the mismatch between school training and real mass-casualty conditions.
